profile.name: Jon Penn
profile.target_geography: Australia / APAC, with preference for Sydney-based or regionally scoped roles. Open to international advisory, board or strategic roles where the mandate has strong APAC relevance.
profile.desired_next_move: Transition from full-time operating CEO/MD roles into a more flexible senior advisory, board, portfolio or strategic operating role across media, entertainment, streaming, FAST, content distribution, IP monetisation or digital media growth.
profile.roles_wanted: Advisory board roles, board director roles, strategic advisor roles, operating partner roles, portfolio company advisor roles, non-executive director roles, or selective CEO/GM mandates where the role involves media growth, streaming, FAST, IP commercialisation, content distribution or APAC market expansion.
profile.roles_not_wanted: Full-time corporate roles with heavy internal politics, roles requiring relocation outside APAC, pure advertising sales roles, pure product/technology roles, or roles too similar to the current Blue Ant Media operating mandate.
profile.constraints: Prefers APAC-focused opportunities and ideally Sydney-based. Wants more flexibility than a traditional full-time executive role. Likely strongest where his commercial, relationship-led and market-building experience is valued, rather than in deeply technical product/platform roles.
profile.relationship_assets: Deep relationships across BBC Studios, Blue Ant Media, Fremantle, Foxtel, Nine, Paramount, broadcasters, production companies, distributors, streaming platforms and APAC media executives. Experience building Bluey commercially in APAC, launching/scaling BBC Studios Productions Australia, founding BritBox JV, and scaling Blue Ant Media’s APAC business.
profile.advisor_notes: The client appears to be exploring a transition away from a traditional full-time operating role toward a more flexible advisory/board/portfolio career. The key advisory question is how to package his executive credibility into a clear market proposition: APAC media and entertainment growth advisor with specific strength in streaming, FAST, IP monetisation, content distribution and regional commercial expansion. Avoid positioning him too generically as a “media executive”. Test whether the strongest path is advisory/board roles, operating partner work, or a selective CEO/GM mandate attached to a high-growth media/streaming asset.

positioning.executive_positioning: APAC media growth commercialist with 23 years' P&L leadership (A$200m+) in content licensing, streaming launches and IP monetisation. Market reads him as: operator-turned-advisor with rare credibility across broadcasters, streamers and regional distribution networks; strongest where APAC scaling, partnership architecture and content asset commercialisation matter more than product or platform ownership.
core_strengths: APAC streaming and FAST P&L: launched and scaled BritBox Australia, BBC Earth JV, Blue Ant digital channels; demonstrated 40% profit growth at BBC Studios ANZ over three years.
Content IP monetisation at scale: commercialised Bluey to A$1B+ APAC portfolio across 1,000+ product lines; built MasterChef Australia into multi-million-dollar multi-platform property.

Regional partnership and market-entry execution: established offices in Singapore, Mumbai and China; negotiated strategic partnerships with Bilibili, Tencent, ByteDance, Foxtel, ABC and Nine at C-suite level.

APAC network density: deep relationships across BBC Studios, Fremantle, Blue Ant, Foxtel, Nine, SkyNZ, Paramount and major broadcasters/streamers; 180-person regional team leadership across seven offices.
market_credibility: A$200m+ APAC regional P&L management (EVP 2019–2022); A$120m ANZ P&L (MD 2013–2019); demonstrated 40% profit growth over three years at BBC Studios ANZ.
Bluey commercialisation: built A$1B+ APAC property with 1,000+ licensed product lines from zero commercial architecture to full-scale regional IP licensing engine.
BritBox Australia and BBC Studios Productions Australia launch and scaling: streaming service launch and television production company foundation 2017–2019 with broadcaster partnerships (ABC, SkyNZ).
Blue Ant Media APAC: tripled revenue to A$10m within 12 months (2023–2024) demonstrating rapid commercialisation and market-entry capability in emerging APAC territories.
positioning_risks: Advisory track record opacity: 2022–present advisory and board roles (Future of Media Advisory, ACT Media Ventures, Matterworks) show no disclosed client outcomes, revenue impact or strategic wins; market cannot yet validate advisory credibility or deal-making ROI.

Operating mandate fatigue risk: identified as seeking flexibility away from 'full-time corporate roles with heavy internal politics'—hires may read this as fatigue or capability ceiling rather than strategic choice, especially if advisory roles are perceived as partial-engagement.

Founder-to-scale operator perception gap: strongest evidence is in scaled regional distribution and licensing (Fremantle, BBC Studios ANZ)—newer evidence at Blue Ant (A$0–A$10m in 12 months) lacks P&L maturity or multi-year commercial proof, risking perception as turnaround expert rather than strategic growth advisor.

Technology and product platform underweight: explicit avoidance of 'pure product/technology roles' combined with no evidence of product leadership, digital platform strategy or technology partnerships beyond streaming carriage deals; limits positioning in high-growth tech-enabled media/streaming executive roles.
positioning.narrative_to_lead: Built three distinct regional streaming and content franchises from market entry to commercial scale (BritBox Australia, BBC Productions Australia, Blue Ant APAC)—demonstrating repeatable playbook for APAC media asset expansion. His core narrative: 'I commercialise regional content and IP at scale through partnership-led distribution and licensing architectures; now advising founders and operators on APAC streaming, FAST and IP monetisation.' This anchors him as practitioner-advisor with skin-in-the-game experience, not theoretical consultant.
positioning.narrative_to_avoid: Framing as generic 'media executive' or 'board advisor' without tying to specific APAC scaling or IP commercialisation mandate invites commoditisation. Avoid passive positioning as 'seeking board roles' or emphasising flexibility/work-life balance over commercial impact—this triggers 'semi-retired' perception and closes doors with growth-stage or PE-backed operators. Do not lead with operational scale (180 staff, A$200m P&L) alone without linking to specific asset commercialisation wins (Bluey, BritBox, Blue Ant revenue triple); markets conflate organisational size with strategic depth.

recommended_pathways.pathway: Operating Partner, Strategic Advisor or Board Director at Growth-Stage APAC Streaming / FAST Platform (Series B–C or PE-backed)
recommended_pathways.rationale: Direct fit: proven ability to launch and scale streaming platforms (BritBox, BBC Earth) and commercial growth via partnership deals; advises on APAC expansion, content licensing and platform P&L; e.g., FAST platform seeking regional distribution or Series B streamer entering APAC.
recommended_pathways.stretch_risk: Must demonstrate value-add beyond current executive title; position as active advisor with capital-allocation or deal-sourcing mandate, not passive director role.

recommended_pathways.pathway: Advisory Board or Strategic Advisor, Content IP Licensing / Monetisation Specialist for Global Media Company or Production Studio
recommended_pathways.rationale: Bluey, MasterChef, QI licensing track record positions him as rare APAC IP commercialisation expert; global studios and production companies (Fremantle, Disney, Warner Bros) expanding APAC IP licensing need practitioner counsel.
recommended_pathways.stretch_risk: Must package advisory mandate as structured: commercial licensing strategy, regional partnership architecture, product-line expansion models—not vague 'strategic advisor' title without defined scope or success metrics.

recommended_pathways.pathway: Operating Partner or Portfolio Advisor to Growth-Stage or Turnaround Media / Entertainment Portfolio Company (PE-backed or VC-backed)
recommended_pathways.rationale: P&L scale (A$200m+), multi-market operational experience and partnership network suit PE value-creation playbooks; selective CEO/GM mandate also possible if attached to streaming, IP or content distribution growth asset.
recommended_pathways.stretch_risk: PE firms expect board-level financial restructuring, capital allocation and M&A experience—advisor must evidence deal sourcing, capital raising or investment strategy; otherwise risk marginalisation as operational tactician.

recommended_pathways.pathway: Selective CEO / GM Mandate: High-Growth Streaming, FAST or Content Distribution Platform Expansion (APAC Mandate or Corporate Development Role)
recommended_pathways.rationale: Still open to CEO/GM roles tied to streaming, FAST or IP monetisation growth. Fits if role is scoped to regional commercial expansion and partnership-led growth rather than end-to-end platform technology build.
recommended_pathways.stretch_risk: Credibly repositions as 'returning to operating role only for right asset' if mandate is genuinely growth-scoped and APAC-centric; otherwise signals inconsistency with stated flexibility goal. Requires demonstrable strategic fit with specific asset.

advisor_only_notes: Advisory credentials gap is the key vulnerability: no disclosed outcomes or client wins from 2022–present advisory roles (Future of Media, ACT Media Ventures, Matterworks). Before pitching for board or advisory roles, secure 1–2 specific case studies or commercial outcomes (deal closed, revenue generated, company funded/exited) to close 'credential credibility' questions. Generic advisory positioning will not move the needle vs. operating peers.

Negotiate from multiple angles simultaneously: position as (a) APAC IP monetisation specialist for global studios, (b) operating partner for PE/VC-backed growth-stage streamers, (c) selective MD for streaming/FAST/distribution asset with regional mandate. Do not anchor initially as 'board director seeker'—it commoditises and signals secondary priority. Instead, anchor as outcome-focused advisor willing to operate selectively if the asset and mandate fit.

Watch for 'fatigue narrative' in market feedback: interviewers may interpret preference for 'flexible roles' and explicit rejection of 'heavy internal politics' and 'full-time corporate' as burn-out or capability ceiling. Counter pre-emptively: frame as deliberate evolution toward impact (advisory/board counsel where strategic leverage is higher) and portfolio diversification, not retreat.

Relationship leverage is under-optimised: Foxtel, Nine, Paramount, SkyNZ, Fremantle, broadcasters, Bilibili, Tencent partnerships represent significant deal-sourcing and anchor-client capital. Likely high-ROI to formalize 'trusted advisor to growth-stage media companies' pitch through three legacy relationships and track 1–2 transaction leads or portfolio introductions in first 90 days post-transition; demonstrates operating partner credibility.

role_patterns: Consistent elevation into regional P&L ownership and team leadership across expanding geographic scope (from ANZ/Asia silos to unified APAC structure)
Operator-to-advisor trajectory evident: hybrid MD + advisor 2022–present suggests intentional pivot toward counsel and strategic input roles
Specialisation in emerging business model launches and innovation (streaming platforms, production companies, new channels, live events adaptation)
Strong track record of partnership-led growth rather than organic build: Foxtel, Sky NZ, Fetch, ABC, SBS, China platforms, India JV
commercial_strengths: Content licensing and IP monetisation: Bluey ($1B+ APAC portfolio, 1,000+ product lines), MasterChef Australia multi-platform expansion, Australian Idol commercial development
Broadcaster and streaming platform partnerships: Foxtel carriage deals, ABC output agreements, Fetch launch partnerships, Sky NZ negotiation, Paramount and Bilibili/Tencent/ByteDance strategic deals
Subscription television and FAST channel P&L: 7 owned BBC channels (Living, First), multi-platform distribution and carriage negotiation
Revenue growth and cost optimisation: 40% profit increase over three years at BBC Studios ANZ; triple revenue scale at Blue Ant (A$0–A$10m in 12 months)
transformation_strengths: Organisational restructuring: merged ANZ and Asia regional divisions into unified 180-person APAC business unit with consolidated P&L governance (2019)
Cultural transformation: relocated BBC Studios Australia office, achieved highest engagement scores in BBC group globally (2018)
Business model innovation: founded BBC Studios Productions Australia (TV production), BritBox Australia (streaming), and JumpStart (innovation training programme)
Team refresh and strategic realignment: renewed team approach to China market, new regional heads, regional services centre restructuring
evidence_gaps: Advisory business impact (2022–present): no client revenue figures, deal outcomes, or outcome metrics provided for Future of Media Advisory or current advisory board roles (ACT Media Ventures, Matterworks)
Strategic advisory depth: no evidence of board-level counsel, investor relations, M&A advisory, or financial restructuring work—primarily operational MD experience claimed
Competitive benchmarking: no market share data, sector positioning analysis, or competitor intelligence evidenced beyond internal growth metrics
Investor relations and capital raising: no evidence of equity raise, investment strategy, or capital allocation leadership
under_positioned_assets: Cultural and organisational leadership: highest BBC group engagement scores globally (2018) underemphasised; relevant to advisor positioning on people-centric transformation

Business innovation and incubation: JumpStart innovation training programme and track record of founding new business lines (BBC Productions, BritBox) position him well for advisory work on internal ventures

Strategic market navigation: experience opening offices in emerging markets (Mumbai, Singapore), navigating China partnerships (Bilibili, Tencent, ByteDance), and India JV structuring indicate international business development acumen not highlighted

Partnership and ecosystem building: consistent track record forging high-value broadcaster, streamer, and tech platform partnerships (Foxtel, ABC, Fetch, SkyNZ, Paramount, Bilibili) demonstrates relationship capital underutilised in advisory framing
cv_improvement_recommendations: Add quantified outcomes for Future of Media Advisory (2022–present): specify client names, deal values, revenue impacted, or strategic outcomes delivered to credibly position advisory capabilities
Elevate strategic advisory experience: reframe or add evidence of board-level counsel, M&A involvement, or sector strategy work to justify transition from operator to advisor credibly
Emphasise innovation and business model leadership: dedicate a section to business incubation track record (BBC Productions, BritBox, JumpStart) to support advisory positioning on transformation
Quantify partnership ecosystem value: add metrics on strategic partnerships (Bilibili, Tencent, ByteDance, BBC Earth JV) to evidence relationship capital and deal-making depth for advisor credibility
Strengthen market insights narrative: add evidence of thought leadership, external speaking, industry board roles (ASTRA noted), or sector foresight to position as credible strategic advisor
Clarify advisory scope and current role: current role (MD Blue Ant, 2023–present) conflicts with stated advisor pivot; clarify part-time/strategic advisor role and time commitment
Simplify and strengthen opening summary: reduce generic language ('outstanding', 'exceptional'); lead with specific commercial outcomes and deal-making depth relevant to advisory value proposition
advisor_only_notes: Role ambiguity risk: CV shows MD Blue Ant Media (2023–present) alongside advisory board memberships; unclear if MD is full-time or strategic/advisory. Advisor client will need clarity on whether transition to advisory is underway or aspiration. This creates perception risk if advisor positioning is marketed before operational role concludes.

Advisory business opacity: Future of Media Advisory (2022–present) shows no revenue, client outcomes, or scope. If this is the 'proof of advisory concept', it lacks evidence. Clients will question whether advisory is viable business or portfolio activity. Recommend quantifying impact or deprioritising this claim.

Operator-heavy narrative: despite stated desire for advisory role, entire CV is operational (P&L, team leadership, execution). No board governance experience, investor relations, financial restructuring, or strategic counsel work evidenced. Transition to advisor will require demonstrating capability beyond P&L management.

Partnership-led growth pattern: strong at leveraging broadcaster and platform partnerships but no evidence of venture capital, investment thesis, or financial engineering advisory work. If targeting PE/growth equity/venture advisory, this gap is material.

Geographic specialisation: entire career in APAC media. If advisor positioning targets global scale or other sectors (tech, fintech, deeptech), lack of breadth is limiting. If advisory focuses on APAC media/entertainment, this is a strength.

Potential narrative: reposition as 'operator with strategic partnerships and innovation track record seeking to accelerate media/entertainment ventures through board and strategic advisory roles.' This aligns current operator role, advisory boards, and advisory business more coherently than pure 'advisor' positioning.

market_radar.market_summary: APAC streaming and FAST platforms are rapidly scaling investment and content licensing deals (Comscore 2025 identifies FAST as a primary growth engine with 1.8B+ US viewing hours), creating urgent demand for regional growth advisors with proven IP monetisation and distribution partnership track records. The client's rare combination of A$200m+ APAC P&L leadership, Bluey and BritBox launch credentials, and established relationships across broadcasters, streamers and tech platforms positions him as a high-value strategic advisor or operating partner for Series B-C growth-stage APAC media platforms, PE-backed content portfolio companies, and global studios expanding regional IP licensing. Selective CEO/GM mandates tied to streaming expansion or FAST platform commercialisation remain viable if scoped to partnership-led growth rather than end-to-end platform technology build.

priority_pathways.pathway: Operating Partner / Strategic Advisor, Growth-Stage APAC Streaming or FAST Platform (Series B–C or PE-backed)
priority_pathways.why_relevant: Proven track record launching and scaling BritBox Australia and BBC Earth JV; demonstrated ability to commercialise streaming platforms via partnership-led distribution and APAC regional expansion.
priority_pathways.market_pull: FAST channels now command substantial viewer attention (1.8B hours US viewership 2025); APAC streaming platforms expanding aggressively and seeking regional commercial expertise.
priority_pathways.watchouts: Must clarify specific advisory outcomes and deal impact from 2022–present advisory roles to validate strategic value-add; avoid perception as execution-only operator.

priority_pathways.pathway: Advisory Board / Strategic Advisor, Global Content IP Licensing & Monetisation Specialist (Production Studios or Media Corporations)
priority_pathways.why_relevant: Rare APAC IP commercialisation expertise across Bluey ($1B+ portfolio, 1,000+ product lines), MasterChef, and QI; positioned as practitioner-advisor with demonstrated regional licensing playbook.
priority_pathways.market_pull: Global studios (Disney, Warner Bros, Fremantle) expanding APAC IP licensing revenue; licensing ecosystems maturing with increased focus on regional monetisation strategies.
priority_pathways.watchouts: Position as APAC-specific licensing expert, not generic advisory consultant; ensure advisory mandate includes deal origination and outcome accountability.

priority_pathways.pathway: Portfolio Advisor / Operating Partner, PE-backed Media or Entertainment Growth Portfolio (Turnaround or Scale)
priority_pathways.why_relevant: A$200m+ P&L leadership across multiple markets; demonstrated multi-market operational experience and deep partnership network across broadcasters, streamers and distributors suit PE value-creation plays.
priority_pathways.market_pull: APAC PE market expanding; private equity investors targeting media/entertainment diversification and operational improvement; value-creation demand for seasoned portfolio operators.
priority_pathways.watchouts: PE roles require demonstrated financial engineering and exit-focused thinking; operational expertise must translate into measurable value creation metrics and investor communications.

priority_pathways.pathway: Selective CEO / GM Mandate, High-Growth Streaming, FAST or Content Distribution Platform (APAC Regional or Corporate Development Focus)
priority_pathways.why_relevant: Open to CEO/GM roles scoped to regional commercial expansion and partnership-led growth; track record scaling Blue Ant APAC revenue 3x in 12 months demonstrates rapid market-building capability.
priority_pathways.market_pull: FAST and SVOD platforms aggressively building APAC presence; high-growth streaming platforms seeking commercial leaders with regional distribution credibility and platform partnership expertise.
priority_pathways.watchouts: Risk of CEO fatigue misread as capability ceiling; ensure role is explicitly scoped to commercial/partnership growth rather than end-to-end platform technology build or full corporate management.

priority_pathways.pathway: Non-Executive Director, ASX-listed or Growth-Stage Media, Broadcast or Entertainment Company (APAC Strategic Mandate)
priority_pathways.why_relevant: C-suite credibility across BBC global leadership and Nine Entertainment; APAC regional expertise and board-level relationship network position him for NED roles with strategic APAC mandate.
priority_pathways.market_pull: Australia and New Zealand broadcasters and media companies seeking board-level APAC strategy and streaming growth expertise; corporate governance frameworks increasingly value operational credibility.
priority_pathways.watchouts: Board roles require articulated governance mindset and board-level strategic counsel; avoid perception as operational operator filling board seat; demonstrate prior board advisory or NED outcomes.

sources.snippet: ur fifth annual *Forbes Asia* 100 to Watch list provides a window into the vibrant world of small companies and startups in the Asia-Pacific region. advisor_brief.brief_summary: Jon Penn is mid-transition from a full-time operating MD role at Blue Ant Media toward a flexible advisory, board, and portfolio career in APAC media, streaming, FAST, and IP monetisation. His commercial credibility is strong and rare, but his advisory positioning is underdeveloped and lacks evidenced outcomes — this is the single biggest risk to a successful transition. The session must sharpen his market proposition, stress-test his readiness to leave the operating seat, and advance the highest-conviction opportunities before momentum stalls.
advisor_brief.client_situation: Jon is currently MD at Blue Ant Media (2023–present) while simultaneously holding advisory board roles (ACT Media Ventures, Matterworks) and running Future of Media Advisory — creating role ambiguity that the market may already be reading. His stated 6–12 month transition window is live, but no confirmed pipeline of board or advisory mandates is evidenced. The constraint set (APAC-only, no relocation, no full-time corporate, no pure ad sales or tech) is tight and narrows the addressable opportunity set to a defined but competitive universe of APAC streaming, FAST, PE-backed media, and global studio advisory roles.
session_focus: Validate whether the Blue Ant MD role is full-time or strategic/advisory — this has direct bearing on how and when to activate the market.
Agree on a single, commercially sharp positioning statement to lead with — not a menu of options.
Advance the Foxtel warm intro and APAC streaming/FAST operating partner pipeline with specific names and outreach plan.
Pressure-test the advisory track record: identify 2–3 quantified case studies from Future of Media Advisory or board roles before going to market.
Decide whether to anchor as operating partner, board director, or selective CEO/GM — sequencing matters and all three simultaneously risks diluting credibility.
key_positioning_insights: The 'Regional Commercial Scale Architect' archetype is credible and differentiated — but only if anchored to Bluey, BritBox, and Blue Ant revenue triple, not to organisational scale (180 staff, A$200m+ P&L) alone.

The Bluey A$1B+ APAC commercialisation story is his single most powerful market signal — it is rare, outcome-specific, and instantly credible with global studios and IP monetisation buyers; it should lead every conversation.

His entire career is APAC-specialist, which is a strength in the current market context (FAST growth, global studio APAC expansion) but may read as geographic limitation if he pursues global advisory mandates without APAC-anchored framing.

The 'flexibility sought' language is a latent liability — sophisticated hiring decision-makers at PE firms and growth-stage platforms may read this as operating fatigue or capability ceiling rather than strategic portfolio evolution; this framing needs to be actively countered.

Advisory track record opacity (Future of Media Advisory, ACT Media Ventures, Matterworks — no disclosed outcomes) is the most actionable near-term gap: one or two specific deal or impact case studies would materially change how the market assesses his transition readiness.

market_signals_to_discuss: FAST sector is the most active current growth signal: 1.8B+ US viewing hours (Comscore 2025) and emerging as primary streaming growth engine — directly relevant to Jon's Blue Ant FAST channel and BritBox experience.

BBC Studios APAC leadership restructure (ANZ and Asia unified) may have created a relationship or advisory entry point — worth validating whether this creates an opportunity or closes a door given Jon's tenure there.

BritBox International appointed new CEO Reemah Sakaan with explicit mandate to scale globally; APAC expansion is active — this is a time-sensitive warm relationship opportunity that should be activated now, not in 6 months.

PE investment in APAC media and content distribution is intensifying (Pacific Equity Partners, Ares, Bain 2024 APAC PE data); operating partner demand from PE-backed media portfolios is a viable and under-explored pathway for Jon.

Global streaming content spend exceeds $50B annually with IP licensing infrastructure expanding — global studios are building out APAC licensing capability, which is Jon's most differentiated and evidenced skill set.
questions_to_ask_client: What is your current actual time commitment at Blue Ant Media — full-time MD or more of a strategic/advisory mandate? This affects every positioning and timing decision.

Can you name two or three specific commercial outcomes from Future of Media Advisory or your current board roles that you could put in front of a PE firm or studio today as evidence of advisory value?
When you say you want flexibility over a full-time operating role, how do you describe that choice to a PE-backed growth platform who needs operating accountability — what is your framing?
Which of your existing relationships — Foxtel, Fremantle, BBC Studios, Paramount, Bilibili — are genuinely warm and active right now, versus historical contacts who may not reflect current access?
Have you had any advisory, board, or operating partner conversations in the last 90 days that have progressed to a second meeting — and if not, what do you think is stalling them?

If a PE-backed FAST platform offered you an operating partner mandate with real equity upside and a 12-month performance commitment, would you take it — or is the flexibility constraint a firm boundary?
advisor_challenges: The advisory track record (2022–present) is currently unsubstantiated in market-facing terms — without disclosed outcomes, the 'Future of Media Advisory' claim may be doing more harm than good by raising the credibility question.

The stated desire for 'flexibility' and rejection of 'full-time corporate roles with heavy internal politics' is not a market-facing positioning asset — it is a constraint that sophisticated buyers will probe; a stronger counter-narrative is needed.

Jon's CV tells an operator's story, not an advisor's — leading with P&L scale and team size without advisor-specific evidence (deals closed, companies scaled, capital deployed) will not convert the advisory pivot in competitive situations.

The Blue Ant APAC revenue triple (A$0–A$10m in 12 months) is compelling but risks positioning him as an early-stage turnaround specialist rather than a strategic growth advisor — needs to be framed carefully against the larger BBC Studios and Fremantle track records.
recommended_next_actions: Build a shortlist of 8–12 APAC FAST and streaming platforms at Series B–C with PE backing; map investor and board decision-makers for warm outreach within 30 days.
Identify and prepare a targeted outreach brief for BritBox International CEO Reemah Sakaan — time-sensitive given active APAC expansion mandate.
Map current board and advisory roles (ACT Media Ventures, Matterworks, Future of Media Advisory) to 2–3 specific commercial outcomes or case studies suitable for market-facing use.

Research PE-backed APAC media portfolios (Pacific Equity Partners, Ares, Bain APAC) and identify operating partner or board director openings; prepare a PE-specific value proposition framed around P&L and partnership track record.
Validate whether any contractual constraints from Blue Ant Media or BBC Studios limit Jon's ability to approach Fremantle, Paramount, or BritBox in an advisory capacity.
advisor_only_notes: The Blue Ant MD role ambiguity is a timing and credibility risk — if Jon is marketing himself as an advisor-in-transition while holding a full-time operating title, sophisticated buyers will question his availability and commitment; this needs to be resolved or explicitly managed in positioning.

There is a plausible 'burn-out or ceiling' read in the market that Jon may be unaware of — the combination of leaving a full-time role, emphasising flexibility, and lacking disclosed advisory wins creates a narrative that growth-stage and PE-backed operators will flag; the advisor should raise this directly.

Jon's relationship capital with Foxtel, Fremantle, BBC Studios, and Bilibili is potentially his most valuable near-term asset, but there is no evidence he has formally activated any of these for deal-sourcing or advisory anchor-client development — this represents an under-optimised and time-sensitive opportunity.

The Bluey A$1B+ claim should be validated before being used as a primary market credential — if this figure reflects total property valuation or retail sales rather than Jon's directly attributable commercial contribution, sophisticated buyers (PE firms, global studios) may probe the attribution and undermine credibility if the framing is not precise.
